QF.id: VII Introduction to Securities Broking Operations_3

question.text: Straight Through Processing (STP) for institutional trades was mandated by SEBI in which year?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** In 2004, SEBI mandated that all institutional trades executed on Stock Exchanges should be processed through STP. STP automates end-to-end processing from front to back office and general ledger, reducing manual entry, errors, and settlement time. **The Trap:** STP is specifically for institutional trades (which involve custodians), not retail trades which follow a different settlement path. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series V covers STP in the context of clearing and settlement efficiency. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** STP = one-pass processing from deal to settlement. Benefits: compressed settlement time, fewer errors, lower costs, better audit trail. Key for institutional clients who use custodians for settlement.
